theme.name: 1960s NYC Chinatown Vintage
theme.vibe: Cinematic, tactical, smoky, high-stakes real estate, tactile vintage
theme.background_texture: Film grain or subtle parchment/noise overlay mandatory to avoid digital flatness.

background.usage: Global app background, lobbies, login screen.

primary.name: Vintage Brass/Gold
primary.usage: Primary CTAs, active player borders, highlighted tiles, key UI interactions.

secondary.name: Mahogany Red
secondary.usage: Negative actions, alerts, specific board sections.

accent.name: Jade Green
accent.usage: Positive income, successful trades, completion indicators.

hierarchy.h1: text-4xl sm:text-5xl font-serif tracking-tight text-amber-50 leading-tight

typesetting_rules: Headings (H1/H2) MUST use the serif font (Cormorant Garamond) with tight letter spacing.
Overlines and small labels MUST be uppercase, text-xs, with wide spacing (tracking-[0.2em]).
Numeric values (cash, round/year 1965-1970, tile IDs) MUST use the monospace font for a ledger/accounting aesthetic.
Avoid 'Inter' and standard defaults. We want this to feel like a vintage document or game box.

layout_and_spacing.system: Generous Spacing (p-4, p-6, p-8) for panels, precise density for the board.
layout_and_spacing.grid_bento: Control Room Grid strategy for dashboards/panels. Dense UI utilizing all space without empty gaping holes.
layout_and_spacing.board_layout: The Game Board is the central focus. Since it involves 85 tiles overlaid on an image, the main board container must use `position: relative` and allow absolute placement based on percentage coordinates.
layout_and_spacing.player_panels: Located to the sides or bottom depending on viewport. Must use tactile neumorphic edges or solid borders to resemble physical paper/cards.
rules: Mobile strategy: Implement a wrapper with `overflow-auto` or `react-zoom-pan-pinch` to let players pan around the large board on smaller screens.
Containers with borders MUST have internal padding (e.g., p-4 or p-6) to prevent content collision.
Do NOT center everything. Use asymmetry to create visual tension and direct the eye to the active player.

surfaces_and_micro_themes.modals_and_overlays: Glassmorphism (Crystal) - `bg-black/80 backdrop-blur-xl backdrop-saturate-150 border border-white/10 shadow-[0_8px_32px_rgba(0,0,0,0.5)]`. Use for Deal Windows, Settings, and Shop Tile selection.
surfaces_and_micro_themes.cards_and_tiles: Neumorphism (Tactile) - Solid `stone-900` background with a `1px border-white/5` and an `amber-500/20` glow on hover.
surfaces_and_micro_themes.active_player_indicator: The 'Tracing Beam' - A glowing amber gradient border or highlight indicating whose turn it is in the player list.

motion_and_interactions.hover_states: Interactive cards and board tiles lift (`-translate-y-1`) and gain an amber shadow (`shadow-[0_4px_12px_rgba(245,158,11,0.2)]`). text color must shift for better contrast.
motion_and_interactions.drag_and_drop: Tiles being dragged should scale up (`scale-105`) and rotate slightly (`rotate-3`) to simulate physical pickup.
motion_and_interactions.turn_transitions: Staggered fade-in (`opacity-0` to `opacity-100` with `translate-y-4`) when new cards/tiles are dealt.

component_rules.html_tailwind: Use raw HTML + Tailwind for the main Game Board, Player Panels, Tile Grid, and Drag-and-Drop Zones. Shadcn is too rigid for the custom board overlay and complex draggable inventory.
component_rules.shadcn: Use heavily customized Shadcn components for Forms, Dropdowns, Dialogs (Trade Windows), and Tooltips.
component_rules.customizations: Override Shadcn's default Vercel-like borders with `amber-500/30`. Replace generic shadows with deep dark shadows. Strip 'ring-offset-background' to match dark stone backgrounds.
component_rules.icons: Use `lucide-react` but set `strokeWidth={1.5}` to make them feel more elegant and vintage, less bulky.

image_urls.description: Cinematic 1960s Chinatown gaming room background for the login and lobby pages.
Vintage ledger/cash register texture used selectively in side panels or scoreboards.
The primary board image. Must be imported from user-provided assets (BOARD.png).

accessibility.contrast: Ensure minimum 4.5:1 ratio for text. Amber-50 on stone-950 is highly accessible. Do not use dark red on black for critical text.
accessibility.focus_states: Focus states must have HIGHER contrast, utilizing `focus:ring-2 focus:ring-amber-400`.
accessibility.testing: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include `data-testid` attributes (e.g., `data-testid='tile-42'`, `data-testid='deal-window'`, `data-testid='accept-trade-button'`).

instructions_to_main_agent: ASSET USAGE: Rely heavily on the provided BOARD.png and the 12 shop tiles (e.g. 30pzorvl_ANTIQUES.png) located in the project assets. DO NOT use placeholders.

BOARD TILES OVERLAY (CRITICAL): The board has 85 numbered tiles spread across 6 sections. You MUST map these 85 IDs (1-85) to relative (%) coordinates over BOARD.png. Create a configuration array of coordinates `[{id: 1, top: '10.5%', left: '15.2%'}, ...]`. Do not use a CSS Grid for this; use absolute positioning inside a relative container so it scales responsively.

DRAG AND DROP (TRADING): The trading system is the core mechanic. Implement robust drag-and-drop. Players should drag shop tiles and properties into a 'Deal Window'. Consider using an HTML5 DnD wrapper or simple mouse tracking combined with Framer Motion. The Deal Window should clearly split 'My Offer' vs 'Their Offer'.

RESPONSIVENESS / VIEWPORT: The board is massive. Implement a wrapper with `overflow-auto` or use a library like `react-zoom-pan-pinch` so players can zoom and pan around the board on smaller screens. The side panels (player stats, hand) should remain fixed or docked.

STATE & SOCKETS: The game loop (6 rounds tracking years 1965-1970) requires tight real-time state tracking via Socket.io. Keep UI presentation components (board, tiles, panels) completely isolated from socket logic via React hooks/context.
JAVASCRIPT NOT TYPESCRIPT: The project is strictly JavaScript (.js/.jsx), NOT TypeScript. Do not write types or interfaces in the code.
TESTING ATTRIBUTES: You MUST add `data-testid` attributes to absolutely every button, tile, input, and modal to ensure the testing agent can interact with the game.
GLOBAL TEXTURE: Apply a subtle CSS noise or SVG filter overlay to the main app container to simulate film grain or vintage paper texture, removing standard digital flatness.
